tool_input.plan:
# Task 24: Production Optimizations and Error Handling ## Context The Kanban board Express + React app needs production-grade hardening. Currently: - The error handler is a 5-line inline function in `server/index.js` with no error typing or logging - The `/health` endpoint returns `{ ok: true }` with no database connectivity test - CORS in production is `origin: false` (no cross-origin support, no configurable allow-list) - No request logging (morgan), no rate limiting, no security headers (helmet) - Vite config has no manual chunk splitting or lazy loading - React has no `ErrorBoundary` component; loading states exist only at the board level All work follows TDD: **write failing tests first, then implement**. --- ## Subtask 1 — Comprehensive Error Handling Middleware ### Files to create/modify - **CREATE** `server/middleware/errorHandler.js` - **CREATE** `server/test/errorHandler.test.mjs` - **MODIFY** `server/index.js` (wire middleware, add ValidationError support) ### Tests to write first (`server/test/errorHandler.test.mjs`) ``` describe('errorHandler middleware') ✗ returns { error, code } shape on any error ✗ uses err.status / err.statusCode for HTTP status (default 500) ✗ returns 400 + code VALIDATION_ERROR for ValidationError ✗ returns 404 + code NOT_FOUND for NotFoundError ✗ returns 500 + code DATABASE_ERROR for DatabaseError ✗ sanitizes message in production (no stack trace in body) ✗ includes stack in body only in development describe('404 handler') ✗ unmatched routes return 404 JSON { error, code: 'NOT_FOUND' } describe('/dev/error route with new middleware') ✗ status 500, body.code === 'INTERNAL_ERROR' ✗ custom err.status respected ``` ### Implementation **`server/middleware/errorHandler.js`** - Export `notFoundHandler(req, res, next)` — creates a `NotFoundError` and passes to next - Export `errorHandler(err, req, res, next)` — 4-argument Express error handler: - Maps error types to HTTP codes: - `ValidationError` → 400, code `VALIDATION_ERROR` - `NotFoundError` → 404, code `NOT_FOUND` - `DatabaseError` / `ForeignKeyError` → 500, code `DATABASE_ERROR` - Others → use `err.status || err.statusCode || 500`, code `INTERNAL_ERROR` - Response shape: `{ error: string, code: string }` - In development: logs `console.error(err.stack)`; may include stack in body - In production: logs sanitized message only; body never includes stack **`server/index.js` changes** - Import `notFoundHandler` and `errorHandler` from `./middleware/errorHandler.js` - Add `ValidationError` class export so routes can use it (or define it in errorHandler.js and re-export) - Replace the inline 404 handler and inline error handler with the imported middleware - Keep existing `NotFoundError`, `DatabaseError`, `ForeignKeyError` from `db/queries.js` **Update `server/test/server.test.mjs`** — adjust existing tests that assert `{ error: 'Not Found' }` to accept the new shape `{ error: ..., code: 'NOT_FOUND' }`. --- ## Subtask 2 — Request Logging with Morgan ### Files to create/modify - **CREATE** `server/middleware/logger.js` - **CREATE** `server/test/logger.test.mjs` - **MODIFY** `server/index.js` (add morgan middleware before routes) - **MODIFY** `server/package.json` (add `morgan` dependency) ### Install ``` npm install morgan --prefix server ``` ### Tests to write first (`server/test/logger.test.mjs`) ``` describe('morgan request logging') ✗ package.json lists morgan as a dependency ✗ morgan can be imported ✗ in development mode, GET /health produces a log line to stdout ✗ in production mode, a log line is emitted (combined format) describe('logger utility') ✗ logger.info(msg) writes a structured line with timestamp and level ✗ logger.error(msg, err) includes error message ✗ logger.warn(msg) emits at warn level ``` ### Implementation **`server/middleware/logger.js`** - Export `createMorganMiddleware()` — returns `morgan('dev')` in development, `morgan('combined')` in production - Export a simple `logger` object with `info`, `warn`, `error` methods that write structured JSON or coloured lines to stdout/stderr based on `NODE_ENV` **`server/index.js` changes** - Import and call `createMorganMiddleware()` as the **first** middleware (before cors/json/routes) --- ## Subtask 3 — Rate Limiting and Security Headers ### Files to create/modify - **CREATE** `server/middleware/security.js` - **CREATE** `server/test/security.test.mjs` - **MODIFY** `server/index.js` (add helmet + rate limiting) - **MODIFY** `server/package.json` (add `helmet`, `express-rate-limit` dependencies) ### Install ``` npm install helmet express-rate-limit --prefix server ``` ### Tests to write first (`server/test/security.test.mjs`) ``` describe('security dependencies') ✗ helmet can be imported ✗ express-rate-limit can be imported ✗ package.json lists helmet ✗ package.json lists express-rate-limit describe('helmet security headers') ✗ GET /health includes X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff ✗ GET /health includes X-Frame-Options header ✗ GET /health does NOT expose X-Powered-By: Express describe('API rate limiting') ✗ after N requests within window, returns HTTP 429 ✗ 429 response includes Retry-After header ✗ 429 body is JSON with { error, code: 'RATE_LIMITED' } ✗ /health endpoint is NOT rate-limited (separate limiter config) ``` ### Implementation **`server/middleware/security.js`** - Export `createHelmetMiddleware()` — returns `helmet()` with default settings - Export `createApiRateLimiter(options)` — returns an `express-rate-limit` instance: - Default: `windowMs: 15 * 60 * 1000` (15 min), `max: 100` - Configurable via `options` for testability (e.g. `max: 5, windowMs: 1000`) - Custom handler returns `{ error: 'Too many requests', code: 'RATE_LIMITED' }` with `Retry-After` header - `standardHeaders: true`, `legacyHeaders: false` - Export `createStrictRateLimiter(options)` — tighter limit for mutation endpoints (POST/PATCH/DELETE) **`server/index.js` changes** - Add `createHelmetMiddleware()` early (after morgan, before cors) - Add `createApiRateLimiter()` scoped to `/api` routes (not `/health`) - Keep `/health` exempt from rate limiting --- ## Subtask 4 — Vite Bundle Optimization (Code Splitting) ### Files to create/modify - **MODIFY** `client/vite.config.js` (add `build.rollupOptions.output.manualChunks`) - **MODIFY** `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx` (lazy-load heavy BlockNote editor) - **CREATE** `client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.jsx` and `LoadingSpinner.css` (used as Suspense fallback) - Simple spinner: `
…

Critical Files Summary | File | Action | |------|--------| | `server/index.js` | Modify — wire all new middleware, enhance health/ready/CORS | | `server/middleware/errorHandler.js` | Create — typed error handler + 404 handler | | `server/middleware/logger.js` | Create — morgan factory + app logger | | `server/middleware/security.js` | Create — helmet + rate limiter factories | | `server/package.json` | Modify — add morgan, helmet, express-rate-limit | | `server/test/errorHandler.test.mjs` | Create — TDD tests for error middleware | | `server/test/logger.test.mjs` | Create — TDD tests for logging | | `server/test/security.test.mjs` | Create — TDD tests for helmet + rate limiting | | `server/test/health.test.mjs` | Create — TDD tests for enhanced health/ready | | `server/test/server.test.mjs` | Modify — update assertions for new error shape | | `client/vite.config.js` | Modify — add manualChunks for code splitting | | `client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.jsx` | Create — class-based error boundary | | `client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.test.jsx` | Create — TDD tests for error boundary | | `client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.jsx` | Create — reusable loading spinner | | `client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.css` | Create — spinner styles | | `client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.test.jsx` | Create — TDD tests for spinner | | `client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx` | Modify — use LoadingSpinner | | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx` | Modify — lazy-load BlockEditor with Suspense | | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx` | Modify — Suspense-aware test | | `client/src/App.jsx` | Modify — wrap Board in ErrorBoundary | | `client/src/App.test.jsx` | Modify — test ErrorBoundary integration |

1. **Existing health test will break** - `server/test/server.test.mjs` line 165-169 has:
```js
it('returns { ok: true }', async () => {
  const res = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/health`);
  const body = await res.json();
  assert.deepStrictEqual(body, { ok: true });
});
```
The new health check returns `{ ok: true, db: 'ok', uptime: number, timestamp: string }`. 

`deepStrictEqual` with `{ ok: true }` will fail because extra fields make it not strictly equal. **This must be fixed in the plan.**

2. **WebSocket status in health check** - The task says to test "WebSocket server status". The broadcaster.js module has module-level state (`wss`, `clients`). There's no exported function to check WS status. The plan needs to either add a `getWsStatus()` export to broadcaster.js or handle this differently.

3. **Missing metrics endpoint** - Task explicitly says "Add metrics endpoint for monitoring system performance and resource usage". Plan has no `/metrics` endpoint.

4. **`getDb` import missing** - The plan references `getDb` but it's not imported in the health check implementation.

5. **`ValidationError` definition location** - Unclear whether this should live in `server/middleware/errorHandler.js` or a separate `server/errors.js` file.

6. **Middleware ordering** - Need to clarify the exact sequence: morgan, helmet, cors, and the rate limiter scoped to `/api`.

7. **Log rotation and file output** - Task requires production logging with rotation, but the plan doesn't address this.

8. **Request size limits and timeouts** - Task specifies these configurations, but they're missing from the plan.

9. **CardModal lazy loading test issue** - The test already mocks `BlockEditor`, so I need to check what the actual issue is with the lazy loading setup. ...)`. When BlockEditor becomes a `React.lazy` import, this mock needs to be updated. Since `vi.mock` is hoisted, the dynamic import from `React.lazy` in the module would use the mocked version. However, the mock path would now be for the lazy import which resolves from `CardModal.jsx`. The mock path `'../CardModal/BlockEditor.jsx'` is relative to the test file. When using `React.lazy(() => import('../CardModal/BlockEditor.jsx'))` in `CardModal.jsx`, the path from `CardModal.jsx`'s perspective is `../CardModal/BlockEditor.jsx`. But from the test file's perspective, the mock would need to match the resolved module path. This is a subtle issue that needs to be addressed.

Both the import in `CardModal.jsx` and the mock in the test file resolve to the same module path, so Vitest should pick up the mock correctly. The real issue is that `React.lazy` with `Suspense` requires wrapping the component in a `<Suspense>` boundary during tests, which the current test setup doesn't have.

Now I'm reviewing the remaining issues in the plan. The CORS configuration only addresses origins but the task explicitly mentions production-specific methods and headers. 1 # Task 24: Production Optimizations and Error Handling 2 3 ## Context 4 5 The Kanban board Express + React app needs production-grade hardening. Currently: 6 - The error handler is a 5-line inline function in `server/index.js` with no error typing or logging 7 - The `/health` endpoint returns `{ ok: true }` with no database connectivity test 8 - CORS in production is `origin: false` (no cross-origin support, no configurable allow-list) 9 - No request logging (morgan), no rate limiting, no security headers (helmet) 10 - Vite config has no manual chunk splitting or lazy loading 11 - React has no `ErrorBoundary` component; loading states exist only at the board level 12 13 All work follows TDD: **write failing tests first, then implement**. 14 15 --- 16 17 ## Subtask 1 — Comprehensive Error Handling Middleware 18 19 ### Files to create/modify 20 - **CREATE** `server/middleware/errorHandler.js` 21 - **CREATE** `server/test/errorHandler.test.mjs` 22 - **MODIFY** `server/index.js` (wire middleware, add ValidationError support) 23 24 ### Tests to write first (`server/test/errorHandler.test.mjs`) 25 26 ``` 27 describe('errorHandler middleware') 28 ✗ returns { error, code } shape on any error 29 ✗ uses err.status / err.statusCode for HTTP status (default 500) 30 ✗ returns 400 + code VALIDATION_ERROR for ValidationError 31 ✗ returns 404 + code NOT_FOUND for NotFoundError 32 ✗ returns 500 + code DATABASE_ERROR for DatabaseError 33 ✗ sanitizes message in production (no stack trace in body) 34 ✗ includes stack in body only in development 35 36 describe('404 handler') 37 ✗ unmatched routes return 404 JSON { error, code: 'NOT_FOUND' } 38 39 describe('/dev/error route with new middleware') 40 ✗ status 500, body.code === 'INTERNAL_ERROR' 41 ✗ custom err.status respected 42 ``` 43 44 ### Implementation 45 46 **`server/middleware/errorHandler.js`** 47 - Export `notFoundHandler(req, res, next)` — creates a `NotFoundError` and passes to next 48 - Export `errorHandler(err, req, res, next)` — 4-argument Express error handler: 49 - Maps error types to HTTP codes: 50 - `ValidationError` → 400, code `VALIDATION_ERROR` 51 - `NotFoundError` → 404, code `NOT_FOUND` 52 - `DatabaseError` / `ForeignKeyError` → 500, code `DATABASE_ERROR` 53 - Others → use `err.status || err.statusCode || 500`, code `INTERNAL_ERROR` 54 - Response shape: `{ error: string, code: string }` 55 - In development: logs `console.error(err.stack)`; may include stack in body 56 - In production: logs sanitized message only; body never includes stack 57 58 **`server/index.js` changes** 59 - Import `notFoundHandler` and `errorHandler` from `./middleware/errorHandler.js` 60 - Add `ValidationError` class export so routes can use it (or define it in errorHandler.js and re-export) 61 - Replace the inline 404 handler and inline error handler with the imported middleware 62 - Keep existing `NotFoundError`, `DatabaseError`, `ForeignKeyError` from `db/queries.js` 63 64 **Update `server/test/server.test.mjs`** — adjust existing tests that assert `{ error: 'Not Found' }` to accept the new shape `{ error: ..., code: 'NOT_FOUND' }`. 65 66 --- 67 68 ## Subtask 2 — Request Logging with Morgan 69 70 ### Files to create/modify 71 - **CREATE** `server/middleware/logger.js` 72 - **CREATE** `server/test/logger.test.mjs` 73 - **MODIFY** `server/index.js` (add morgan middleware before routes) 74 - **MODIFY** `server/package.json` (add `morgan` dependency) 75 76 ### Install 77 ``` 78 npm install morgan --prefix server 79 ``` 80 81 ### Tests to write first (`server/test/logger.test.mjs`) 82 83 ``` 84 describe('morgan request logging') 85 ✗ package.json lists morgan as a dependency 86 ✗ morgan can be imported 87 ✗ in development mode, GET /health produces a log line to stdout 88 ✗ in production mode, a log line is emitted (combined format) 89 90 describe('logger utility') 91 ✗ logger.info(msg) writes a structured line with timestamp and level 92 ✗ logger.error(msg, err) includes error message 93 ✗ logger.warn(msg) emits at warn level 94 ``` 95 96 ### Implementation 97 98 **`server/middleware/logger.js`** 99 - Export `createMorganMiddleware()` — returns `morgan('dev')` in development, `morgan('combined')` in production 100 - Export a simple `logger` object with `info`, `warn`, `error` methods that write structured JSON or coloured lines to stdout/stderr based on `NODE_ENV` 101 102 **`server/index.js` changes** 103 - Import and call `createMorganMiddleware()` as the **first** middleware (before cors/json/routes) 104 105 --- 106 107 ## Subtask 3 — Rate Limiting and Security Headers 108 109 ### Files to create/modify 110 - **CREATE** `server/middleware/security.js` 111 - **CREATE** `server/test/security.test.mjs` 112 - **MODIFY** `server/index.js` (add helmet + rate limiting) 113 - **MODIFY** `server/package.json` (add `helmet`, `express-rate-limit` dependencies) 114 115 ### Install 116 ``` 117 npm install helmet express-rate-limit --prefix server 118 ``` 119 120 ### Tests to write first (`server/test/security.test.mjs`) 121 122 ``` 123 describe('security dependencies') 124 ✗ helmet can be imported 125 ✗ express-rate-limit can be imported 126 ✗ package.json lists helmet 127 ✗ package.json lists express-rate-limit 128 129 describe('helmet security headers') 130 ✗ GET /health includes X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff 131 ✗ GET /health includes X-Frame-Options header 132 ✗ GET /health does NOT expose X-Powered-By: Express 133 134 describe('API rate limiting') 135 ✗ after N requests within window, returns HTTP 429 136 ✗ 429 response includes Retry-After header 137 ✗ 429 body is JSON with { error, code: 'RATE_LIMITED' } 138 ✗ /health endpoint is NOT rate-limited (separate limiter config) 139 ``` 140 141 ### Implementation 142 143 **`server/middleware/security.js`** 144 - Export `createHelmetMiddleware()` — returns `helmet()` with default settings 145 - Export `createApiRateLimiter(options)` — returns an `express-rate-limit` instance: 146 - Default: `windowMs: 15 * 60 * 1000` (15 min), `max: 100` 147 - Configurable via `options` for testability (e.g. `max: 5, windowMs: 1000`) 148 - Custom handler returns `{ error: 'Too many requests', code: 'RATE_LIMITED' }` with `Retry-After` header 149 - `standardHeaders: true`, `legacyHeaders: false` 150 - Export `createStrictRateLimiter(options)` — tighter limit for mutation endpoints (POST/PATCH/DELETE) 151 152 **`server/index.js` changes** 153 - Add `createHelmetMiddleware()` early (after morgan, before cors) 154 - # Task 24: Production Optimizations and Error Handling ## Context The Kanban board Express + React app needs production-grade hardening. Currently: - The error handler is a 5-line inline function in `server/index.js` with no error typing or logging - The `/health` endpoint returns `{ ok: true }` with no database connectivity test - CORS in production is `origin: false` (no cross-origin support, no configurable allow-list) - No request logging (morgan), no rate limiting, no security headers (helmet) - Vite config has no manual chunk splitting or lazy loading - React has no `ErrorBoundary` component; loading states exist only at the board level All work follows TDD: **write failing tests first, then implement**. --- ## Subtask 1 — Comprehensive Error Handling Middleware ### Files to create/modify - **CREATE** `server/middleware/errorHandler.js` - **CREATE** `server/test/errorHandler.test.mjs` - **MODIFY** `server/index.js` (wire middleware, add ValidationError support) ### Tests to write first (`server/test/errorHandler.test.mjs`) ``` describe('errorHandler middleware') ✗ returns { error, code } shape on any error ✗ uses err.status / err.statusCode for HTTP status (default 500) ✗ returns 400 + code VALIDATION_ERROR for ValidationError ✗ returns 404 + code NOT_FOUND for NotFoundError ✗ returns 500 + code DATABASE_ERROR for DatabaseError ✗ sanitizes message in production (no stack trace in body) ✗ includes stack in body only in development describe('404 handler') ✗ unmatched routes return 404 JSON { error, code: 'NOT_FOUND' } describe('/dev/error route with new middleware') ✗ status 500, body.code === 'INTERNAL_ERROR' ✗ custom err.status respected ``` ### Implementation **`server/middleware/errorHandler.js`** - Export `notFoundHandler(req, res, next)` — creates a `NotFoundError` and passes to next - Export `errorHandler(err, req, res, next)` — 4-argument Express error handler: - Maps error types to HTTP codes: - `ValidationError` → 400, code `VALIDATION_ERROR` - `NotFoundError` → 404, code `NOT_FOUND` - `DatabaseError` / `ForeignKeyError` → 500, code `DATABASE_ERROR` - Others → use `err.status || err.statusCode || 500`, code `INTERNAL_ERROR` - Response shape: `{ error: string, code: string }` - In development: logs `console.error(err.stack)`; may include stack in body - In production: logs sanitized message only; body never includes stack **`server/index.js` changes** - Import `notFoundHandler` and `errorHandler` from `./middleware/errorHandler.js` - Add `ValidationError` class export so routes can use it (or define it in errorHandler.js and re-export) - Replace the inline 404 handler and inline error handler with the imported middleware - Keep existing `NotFoundError`, `DatabaseError`, `ForeignKeyError` from `db/queries.js` **Update `server/test/server.test.mjs`** — adjust existing tests that assert `{ error: 'Not Found' }` to accept the new shape `{ error: ..., code: 'NOT_FOUND' }`. --- ## Subtask 2 — Request Logging with Morgan ### Files to create/modify - **CREATE** `server/middleware/logger.js` - **CREATE** `server/test/logger.test.mjs` - **MODIFY** `server/index.js` (add morgan middleware before routes) - **MODIFY** `server/package.json` (add `morgan` dependency) ### Install ``` npm install morgan --prefix server ``` ### Tests to write first (`server/test/logger.test.mjs`) ``` describe('morgan request logging') ✗ package.json lists morgan as a dependency ✗ morgan can be imported ✗ in development mode, GET /health produces a log line to stdout ✗ in production mode, a log line is emitted (combined format) describe('logger utility') ✗ logger.info(msg) writes a structured line with timestamp and level ✗ logger.error(msg, err) includes error message ✗ logger.warn(msg) emits at warn level ``` ### Implementation **`server/middleware/logger.js`** - Export `createMorganMiddleware()` — returns `morgan('dev')` in development, `morgan('combined')` in production - Export a simple `logger` object with `info`, `warn`, `error` methods that write structured JSON or coloured lines to stdout/stderr based on `NODE_ENV` **`server/index.js` changes** - Import and call `createMorganMiddleware()` as the **first** middleware (before cors/json/routes) --- ## Subtask 3 — Rate Limiting and Security Headers ### Files to create/modify - **CREATE** `server/middleware/security.js` - **CREATE** `server/test/security.test.mjs` - **MODIFY** `server/index.js` (add helmet + rate limiting) - **MODIFY** `server/package.json` (add `helmet`, `express-rate-limit` dependencies) ### Install ``` npm install helmet express-rate-limit --prefix server ``` ### Tests to write first (`server/test/security.test.mjs`) ``` describe('security dependencies') ✗ helmet can be imported ✗ express-rate-limit can be imported ✗ package.json lists helmet ✗ package.json lists express-rate-limit describe('helmet security headers') ✗ GET /health includes X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff ✗ GET /health includes X-Frame-Options header ✗ GET /health does NOT expose X-Powered-By: Express describe('API rate limiting') ✗ after N requests within window, returns HTTP 429 ✗ 429 response includes Retry-After header ✗ 429 body is JSON with { error, code: 'RATE_LIMITED' } ✗ /health endpoint is NOT rate-limited (separate limiter config) ``` ### Implementation **`server/middleware/security.js`** - Export `createHelmetMiddleware()` — returns `helmet()` with default settings - Export `createApiRateLimiter(options)` — returns an `express-rate-limit` instance: - Default: `windowMs: 15 * 60 * 1000` (15 min), `max: 100` - Configurable via `options` for testability (e.g. `max: 5, windowMs: 1000`) - Custom handler returns `{ error: 'Too many requests', code: 'RATE_LIMITED' }` with `Retry-After` header - `standardHeaders: true`, `legacyHeaders: false` - Export `createStrictRateLimiter(options)` — tighter limit for mutation endpoints (POST/PATCH/DELETE) **`server/index.js` changes** - Add `createHelmetMiddleware()` early (after morgan, before cors) - Add `createApiRateLimiter()` scoped to `/api` routes (not `/health`) - Keep `/health` exempt from rate limiting --- ## Subtask 4 — Vite Bundle Optimization (Code Splitting) ### Files to create/modify - **MODIFY** `client/vite.config.js` (add `build.rollupOptions.output.manualChunks`) - **MODIFY** `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx` (lazy-load heavy BlockNote editor) - **CREATE** `client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.jsx` and `LoadingSpinner.css` (used as Suspense fallback) - **MODIFY** `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx` (update for Suspense wrapper if needed) ### Tests to write first Since bundle splitting is a build-time concern, tests focus on **verifiable runtime behaviour** of lazy loading: **`client/src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx`** (extend existing) ``` describe('CardModal lazy loading') ✗ renders Suspense fallback (loading spinner) while BlockEditor is loading ✗ renders BlockEditor content once loaded (async, act()) ``` **`client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.test.jsx`** (new) ``` describe('LoadingSpinner') ✗ renders with aria-label="Loading" ✗ renders spinner element with correct class ``` ### Implementation **`client/vite.config.js` changes** ```js build: { rollupOptions: { output: { manualChunks: { 'vendor-react': ['react', 'react-dom'], 'vendor-dnd': ['@dnd-kit/core', '@dnd-kit/sortable', '@dnd-kit/utilities'], 'vendor-blocknote': ['@blocknote/core', '@blocknote/react'], } } } } ``` **`client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.jsx`** - Simple spinner: `
…
` **`client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx`** changes - Lazy-load `BlockEditor`: `const BlockEditor = React.lazy(() => import('../CardModal/BlockEditor.jsx'))` - Wrap the editor section in `}>…` --- ## Subtask 5 — React Error Boundaries and Loading States ### Files to create/modify - **CREATE** `client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.jsx` - **CREATE** `client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.test.jsx` - **MODIFY** `client/src/App.jsx` (wrap Board in ErrorBoundary) - **MODIFY** `client/src/App.test.jsx` (test ErrorBoundary integration) - **MODIFY** `client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx` (improved loading state using LoadingSpinner) ### Tests to write first **`client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.test.jsx`** (new) ``` describe('ErrorBoundary') ✗ renders children when no error ✗ catches error thrown by a child and renders fallback UI ✗ fallback UI shows user-friendly error message ✗ fallback UI has a "Try again" / retry button ✗ clicking retry resets the boundary and re-renders children ✗ calls onError prop when error is caught (if provided) ``` ### Implementation **`client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.jsx`** - Class component (required for `componentDidCatch`) - State: `{ hasError: false, error: null }` - `static getDerivedStateFromError(error)` — sets `hasError: true` - `componentDidCatch(error, info)` — calls `this.props.onError?.(error, info)`, logs in dev - Fallback UI: styled container with error message + "Try again" button - "Try again" button resets `hasError: false` via `setState` - Accepts optional `fallback` prop for custom fallback (default: built-in UI) **`client/src/App.jsx` changes** ```jsx import ErrorBoundary from './components/ErrorBoundary.jsx' // Wrap in ``` **`client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx` changes** - Replace `
Loading…
` with `` - Import `LoadingSpinner` from `../LoadingSpinner.jsx` --- ## Subtask 6 — Production CORS and Enhanced Health Check ### Files to create/modify - **MODIFY** `server/index.js` (enhance CORS config and health endpoint) - **MODIFY** `server/test/server.test.mjs` (add new health/CORS tests) - **CREATE** `server/test/health.test.mjs` (comprehensive health check tests) ### Tests to write first (`server/test/health.test.mjs`) ``` describe('GET /health — enhanced') ✗ returns 200 with { ok: true, db: 'ok', uptime: number, timestamp: string } ✗ returns Content-Type: application/json ✗ db field is 'ok' when database is reachable (in-memory test DB) ✗ uptime is a positive number (seconds since process start) ✗ timestamp is a valid ISO 8601 date string describe('GET /ready (readiness probe)') ✗ returns 200 { ready: true } when DB is accessible ✗ returns 503 { ready: false, reason: string } when DB is down describe('CORS in production mode with ALLOWED_ORIGINS env var') ✗ allowed origin receives Access-Control-Allow-Origin: ✗ disallowed origin gets no Access-Control-Allow-Origin header ✗ OPTIONS preflight for allowed origin returns 204 ✗ when ALLOWED_ORIGINS is unset in production, falls back to same-origin (no ACAO header) ``` ### Implementation **`server/index.js` changes — CORS** - Replace static `origin: isProduction ? false : true` with a dynamic function: ```js const allowedOrigins = process.env.ALLOWED_ORIGINS ? process.env.ALLOWED_ORIGINS.split(',').map(s => s.trim()) : [] app.use(cors({ origin: isProduction ? (origin, cb) => { if (!origin || allowedOrigins.includes(origin)) cb(null, true) else cb(null, false) } : true, credentials: true, })) ``` **`server/index.js` changes — `/health` endpoint** ```js app.get('/health', (_req, res) => { let dbStatus = 'ok' try { getDb().prepare('SELECT 1').get() } catch { dbStatus = 'error' } res.json({ ok: true, db: dbStatus, uptime: process.uptime(), timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), }) }) ``` **`server/index.js` changes — `/ready` endpoint** ```js app.get('/ready', (_req, res) => { try { getDb().prepare('SELECT 1').get() res.json({ ready: true }) } catch (err) { res.status(503).json({ ready: false, reason: 'Database unavailable' }) } }) ``` Note: The `/health` and `/ready` endpoints must be registered **before** the 404 and error handlers. --- ## Execution Order and Test Commands ### Server tests ```bash # Run all server tests cd server && npm test # Run specific new test files node --test test/errorHandler.test.mjs node --test test/logger.test.mjs node --test test/security.test.mjs node --test test/health.test.mjs # Coverage check npm run test:coverage ``` ### Client tests ```bash # Run all client tests cd client && npm test # Run specific new test files npx vitest run src/components/ErrorBoundary.test.jsx npx vitest run src/components/LoadingSpinner.test.jsx npx vitest run src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx # Coverage check npm run test:coverage ``` ### End-to-end verification 1. `npm run dev` from root — check morgan logs appear in console 2. `curl http://localhost:3001/health` — verify `{ ok: true, db: 'ok', uptime: …, timestamp: … }` 3. `curl http://localhost:3001/ready` — verify `{ ready: true }` 4. `curl -H "Origin: http://evil.com" http://localhost:3001/api/cards` in production mode — verify no ACAO header 5. For the Suspense issue in the tests, since the mocked module resolves synchronously, wrapping the renders in `<Suspense>` will prevent React from throwing an error about a missing boundary, even though the fallback won't actually display.

Actually, I need to reconsider how this works. When `vi.mock` hoists the mock before imports, the `React.lazy` wrapper around the dynamic import will still trigger the Suspense mechanism in React 18, even if the underlying promise resolves immediately—it goes through at least one tick of suspension. In tests, this means the Suspense boundary is necessary to avoid errors.

The practical solution is to wrap all renders in `CardModal.test.jsx` with a `<Suspense>` boundary that has a fallback, then use `await screen.findBy...` or `waitFor` to verify the actual content renders. Alternatively, I could skip `React.lazy` for the Block component entirely to simplify testing. Editor in CardModal. Instead, just use `manualChunks` in Vite to split the bundle. This way the existing tests don't need to change at all. The lazy loading (code splitting) happens at the Vite level, not at the React component level.

Wait, but the task explicitly says "Implement lazy loading for React components using React.lazy() and Suspense." So we need React.lazy.

Option B: Use React.lazy but make the tests work properly. In `CardModal.test.jsx`, wrap all renders with `<Suspense>` and use `waitFor` or `findBy` queries.

Since `vi.mock` makes the module available immediately, with React.lazy in React 18 (not concurrent mode by default in tests), the lazy component might actually render synchronously without showing the Suspense fallback.

Actually, let me reconsider. In React 18 tests, the behavior depends on how the test environment is set up. React Testing Library 16+ uses `createRoot` by default, and with React 18's concurrent features, `React.lazy` + `Suspense` requires wrapping the component with `<Suspense>`. Without it, React throws an error.

The straightforward fix is to wrap all test renders with a Suspense boundary that has a loading fallback. So instead of rendering `CardModal` directly, I'd wrap it in `<Suspense>` with a simple loading div as the fallback.

For the new tests, I need to verify that the lazy loading works. Since the mock resolves synchronously, the lazy component will render without showing the fallback. The test should just verify that the `BlockEditor` content is rendered (by checking for the `data-testid="block-editor"`).

Now I need to write out the full updated plan with all the changes.

For the metrics endpoint, I'm thinking about what data to expose. I can pull process uptime, memory usage stats like heap and RSS, and CPU usage. I'll create an endpoint that returns these metrics as JSON with a timestamp. For log rotation, I need to figure out how to handle file output.

Since the app runs in Docker, the container runtime typically handles log rotation, but the task asks for it explicitly. I could use a package like `rotating-file-stream` to manage file rotation, or pipe morgan output to a write stream. Adding another dependency feels heavy for a simple app, so I'm leaning toward either using date-based filenames with a write stream or just letting Docker handle the rotation.

For request size limits and timeouts, Express has built-in middleware for the former, but timeout handling would need either the `connect-timeout` package or custom logic.

For the probes, I'm thinking about the distinction between liveness and readiness — the task requires both, so I could use `/live` as a minimal check that just confirms the process is running, while `/ready` performs a more thorough check including database connectivity. This aligns with Kubernetes conventions where liveness detects if the container should restart and readiness determines if it can handle traffic.

On CORS, I need to be explicit about allowed methods and headers rather than relying on defaults. For middleware ordering, logging should come first to capture everything, then security headers, CORS configuration, body parsing, and finally the route handlers — this ensures each layer can do its job without interference.

2. **WebSocket status omitted from health check** — task explicitly requires it; needs a `getWsStatus()` export from `broadcaster.js`.
3. **Missing `/metrics` endpoint** — task explicitly says "Add metrics endpoint for monitoring system performance and resource usage".
4. **`getDb` not imported in `server/index.js`** — health/ready endpoints call `getDb()` but it's not currently imported there.
5. **`ValidationError` location undefined** — plan says "or define it in errorHandler.js" without deciding.
6. **Middleware ordering not specified** — multiple subtasks add middleware with conflicting "insert here" instructions.
7. **Log rotation/file output missing** — subtask 2 explicitly requires "Configure log rotation and file output for production".

# Task 24: Production Optimizations and Error Handling ## Context The Kanban board Express + React app needs production-grade hardening. Currently: - The error handler is a 5-line inline function in `server/index.js` with no error typing or logging - The `/health` endpoint returns `{ ok: true }` with no database connectivity or WS status test - CORS in production is `origin: false` (no configurable allow-list, no methods/headers config) - No request logging (morgan), no rate limiting, no security headers (helmet) - Vite config has no manual chunk splitting or lazy loading - React has no `ErrorBoundary` component; loading states exist only at the board level All work follows TDD: **write failing tests first, then implement**. --- ## Final Middleware Order in `server/index.js` All subtasks modify `server/index.js`. The **final** middleware order must be: ``` 1. morgan (request logging) — createMorganMiddleware() 2. helmet (security headers) — createHelmetMiddleware() 3. cors (CORS headers) — cors({ origin, credentials, methods, allowedHeaders }) 4. express.json({ limit: '1mb' }) — body parsing + size limit 5. /api routes with rate limiter — app.use('/api', createApiRateLimiter(), commentsRouter, cardsRouter) 6. /health route — no rate limiter 7. /ready route — no rate limiter 8. /live route — no rate limiter 9. /metrics route — no rate limiter 10. static files (production only) 11. notFoundHandler — from middleware/errorHandler.js 12. errorHandler — from middleware/errorHandler.js (4-arg) ``` --- ## Subtask 1 — Comprehensive Error Handling Middleware ### Files to create/modify - **CREATE** `server/middleware/errorHandler.js` - **CREATE** `server/test/errorHandler.test.mjs` - **MODIFY** `server/index.js` (replace inline handlers; wire `notFoundHandler` + `errorHandler`) ### Tests to write first (`server/test/errorHandler.test.mjs`) ``` describe('errorHandler middleware') ✗ returns { error, code } shape on any generic Error ✗ uses err.status / err.statusCode for HTTP status (default 500) ✗ returns 400 + code 'VALIDATION_ERROR' for ValidationError ✗ returns 404 + code 'NOT_FOUND' for NotFoundError ✗ returns 500 + code 'DATABASE_ERROR' for DatabaseError ✗ returns 500 + code 'DATABASE_ERROR' for ForeignKeyError ✗ in production, response body does NOT include a stack field ✗ code is 'INTERNAL_ERROR' for generic Error with no special type describe('404 handler') ✗ unmatched GET returns 404 JSON { error: string, code: 'NOT_FOUND' } describe('/dev/error route with new middleware') ✗ default returns 500 with body.code === 'INTERNAL_ERROR' ✗ ?status=404 returns 404 with body.code === 'INTERNAL_ERROR' (generic err with err.status) ``` ### Implementation **`server/middleware/errorHandler.js`** Define `ValidationError` here (exported for route handlers to use): ```js export class ValidationError extends Error {} ``` Import `NotFoundError`, `DatabaseError`, `ForeignKeyError` from `../db/queries.js`. Export `notFoundHandler(req, res, next)`: - Creates `new NotFoundError('Not Found')` and calls `next(err)` Export `errorHandler(err, req, res, next)` (4-argument): ``` if (err instanceof ValidationError) → 400, code 'VALIDATION_ERROR' if (err instanceof NotFoundError) → 404, code 'NOT_FOUND' if (err instanceof DatabaseError) → 500, code 'DATABASE_ERROR' (covers ForeignKeyError) otherwise → err.status || err.statusCode || 500, code 'INTERNAL_ERROR' ``` Response shape always: `{ error: string, code: string }` — no stack trace in body ever. Side effect: `console.error(err.stack)` in all environments (server-side log, never in response body). **`server/index.js` changes** - Import: `import { notFoundHandler, errorHandler, ValidationError } from './middleware/errorHandler.js'` - Import: also add `getDb` to the existing import from `./db/queries.js`: `import { initDb, getDb } from './db/queries.js'` - Remove the inline `app.use((_req, res) => ...)` 404 handler - Remove the inline `app.use((err, _req, res, _next) => ...)` error handler - Add `app.use(notFoundHandler)` and `app.use(errorHandler)` at positions 11 and 12 per the middleware order above - Export `ValidationError` from `server/index.js` so routes can import from one place (re-export) **`server/test/server.test.mjs` changes (must update existing assertions)** - The existing `it('404 response body has "error" field')` test uses `assert.ok(body.error)` — this still passes (no change needed there) - The existing `it('response body has "error" string field')` uses `assert.strictEqual(typeof body.error, 'string')` — still passes - **No breaking changes** to existing server.test.mjs assertions from this subtask alone; `{ error, code }` is a superset of `{ error }` --- ## Subtask 2 — Request Logging with Morgan ### Files to create/modify - **CREATE** `server/middleware/logger.js` - **CREATE** `server/test/logger.test.mjs` - **MODIFY** `server/index.js` (add morgan as first middleware) - **MODIFY** `server/package.json` (add `morgan`, `rotating-file-stream`) ### Install ``` npm install morgan rotating-file-stream --prefix server ``` ### Tests to write first (`server/test/logger.test.mjs`) ``` describe('morgan dependency') ✗ package.json lists morgan as a dependency ✗ morgan can be imported as ES module describe('createMorganMiddleware()') ✗ returns a function (valid Express middleware) ✗ in development, GET /health produces a log line to stdout (capture stdout, check non-empty) ✗ in production, a log line is emitted in combined format describe('logger utility') ✗ logger.info(msg) writes a line containing the message to stdout ✗ logger.error(msg, err) writes a line containing the message to stderr ✗ logger.warn(msg) writes a line containing the message to stdout ✗ each log line contains a UTC timestamp ``` ### Implementation **`server/middleware/logger.js`** ```js import morgan from 'morgan' import { createWriteStream } from 'node:fs' import { mkdirSync } from 'node:fs' import { join, dirname } from 'node:path' import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url' import { createStream } from 'rotating-file-stream' const __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)) export function createMorganMiddleware() { const isProduction = process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production' if (!isProduction) { return morgan('dev') // coloured output to stdout } // Production: combined format to stdout AND rotating log file const logDir = join(__dirname, '..', 'logs') mkdirSync(logDir, { recursive: true }) const rotatingStream = createStream('access.log', { interval: '1d', // rotate daily path: logDir, size: '10M', // also rotate when file exceeds 10MB }) // Write to both stdout and the rotating file return morgan('combined', { stream: { write(msg) { process.stdout.write(msg) rotatingStream.write(msg) } } }) } // Structured app-level logger const timestamp = () => new Date().toISOString() export const logger = { info: (msg) => process.stdout.write(`[${timestamp()}] INFO ${msg}\n`), warn: (msg) => process.stdout.write(`[${timestamp()}] WARN ${msg}\n`), error: (msg, err) => process.stderr.write(`[${timestamp()}] ERROR ${msg}${err ? ': ' + err.message : ''}\n`), } ``` **`server/index.js` changes** - Import `createMorganMiddleware` from `./middleware/logger.js` - Add `app.use(createMorganMiddleware())` as the **first** `app.use()` call (position 1 per middleware order) --- ## Subtask 3 — Rate Limiting and Security Headers ### Files to create/modify - **CREATE** `server/middleware/security.js` - **CREATE** `server/test/security.test.mjs` - **MODIFY** `server/index.js` (add helmet + rate limiting; add body size limit) - **MODIFY** `server/package.json` (add `helmet`, `express-rate-limit`) ### Install ``` npm install helmet express-rate-limit --prefix server ``` ### Tests to write first (`server/test/security.test.mjs`) ``` describe('security dependencies') ✗ helmet can be imported ✗ express-rate-limit can be imported ✗ package.json lists helmet ✗ package.json lists express-rate-limit describe('helmet security headers') ✗ GET /health response includes X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff ✗ GET /health response includes X-Frame-Options header ✗ GET /health response does NOT include X-Powered-By describe('API rate limiting') ✗ after exceeding the limit, GET /api/cards returns HTTP 429 ✗ 429 response includes Retry-After header ✗ 429 response body is JSON { error: string, code: 'RATE_LIMITED' } ✗ GET /health is NOT blocked after rate limit triggered on /api describe('request body size limit') ✗ POST /dev/echo with body > 1mb returns 413 ``` ### Implementation **`server/middleware/security.js`** ```js import helmet from 'helmet' import rateLimit from 'express-rate-limit' export function createHelmetMiddleware() { return helmet() // enables all default protections; removes X-Powered-By } export function createApiRateLimiter(options = {}) { return rateLimit({ windowMs: options.windowMs ?? 15 * 60 * 1000, // 15 minutes default max: options.max ?? 100, standardHeaders: true, legacyHeaders: false, // Memory store (default) — sufficient for single-instance; Redis would be // needed for multi-instance deployments but is out of scope for this project handler(req, res) { res.status(429).json({ error: 'Too many requests', code: 'RATE_LIMITED' }) }, }) } ``` **`server/index.js` changes** - Import `createHelmetMiddleware`, `createApiRateLimiter` from `./middleware/security.js` - Position 2: `app.use(createHelmetMiddleware())` - Position 4: change `app.use(express.json())` to `app.use(express.json({ limit: '1mb' }))` - Position 5: `app.use('/api', createApiRateLimiter(), commentsRouter, cardsRouter)` - Remove the prior `app.use('/api', commentsRouter)` and `app.use('/api', cardsRouter)` lines - The rate limiter is scoped to `/api` only; `/health`, `/ready`, `/live`, `/metrics` are exempt - No `createStrictRateLimiter` needed — a single limiter for all `/api` routes is sufficient --- ## Subtask 4 — Vite Bundle Optimization (Code Splitting) ### Files to create/modify - **MODIFY** `client/vite.config.js` (add `build.rollupOptions.output.manualChunks`) - **MODIFY** `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx` (lazy-load BlockEditor) - **CREATE** `client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.jsx` + `LoadingSpinner.css` - **MODIFY** `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx` (wrap renders in ``) - **CREATE** `client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.test.jsx` ### Tests to write first **`client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.test.jsx`** (new, write first) ``` describe('LoadingSpinner') ✗ renders an element with role="status" ✗ has aria-label="Loading" ✗ renders an element with class "loading-spinner" ``` **`client/src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx`** (update existing — critical) `CardModal.jsx` will use `React.lazy` to import `BlockEditor`. React requires every lazy component to be inside a `` boundary, even in tests. Without it, React throws. The existing `vi.mock('../CardModal/BlockEditor.jsx', ...)` mock resolves synchronously, so the Suspense fallback will NOT visually appear in tests, but the boundary must exist. **Update every `render(...)` call** in the file to wrap CardModal in Suspense: ```jsx import { Suspense } from 'react' // Change: render() // To: render(Loading}>) ``` Add one new test: ``` describe('CardModal with lazy BlockEditor') ✗ block editor content is rendered (lazy mock resolves synchronously) ``` ### Implementation **`client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.jsx`** ```jsx import './LoadingSpinner.css' export default function LoadingSpinner() { return (
) } ``` **`client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.css`** ```css .loading-spinner { display: flex; justify-content: center; align-items: center; padding: 2rem; } .spinner-ring { display: block; width: 2rem; height: 2rem; border: 3px solid #e2e8f0; border-top-color: #4f46e5; border-radius: 50%; animation: spin 0.75s linear infinite; } @keyframes spin { to { transform: rotate(360deg); } } ``` **`client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx`** changes ```jsx import React, { Suspense, useEffect, useState, useRef } from 'react' import LoadingSpinner from '../LoadingSpinner.jsx' // Remove: import BlockEditor from '../CardModal/BlockEditor.jsx' // Add: const BlockEditor = React.lazy(() => import('../CardModal/BlockEditor.jsx')) // Wrap the BlockEditor usage with: }> ``` **`client/vite.config.js`** changes — add a `build` key: ```js build: { rollupOptions: { output: { manualChunks: { 'vendor-react': ['react', 'react-dom'], 'vendor-dnd': ['@dnd-kit/core', '@dnd-kit/sortable', '@dnd-kit/utilities'], 'vendor-blocknote': ['@blocknote/core', '@blocknote/react'], }, }, }, }, ``` --- ## Subtask 5 — React Error Boundaries and Loading States ### Files to create/modify - **CREATE** `client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.jsx` - **CREATE** `client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.test.jsx` - **MODIFY** `client/src/App.jsx` (wrap Board in ErrorBoundary) - **MODIFY** `client/src/App.test.jsx` (add ErrorBoundary integration tests) - **MODIFY** `client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx` (use LoadingSpinner for loading state) ### Tests to write first **`client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.test.jsx`** (new) React Testing Library emits `console.error` when a component throws inside an error boundary during tests. Suppress it with `vi.spyOn(console, 'error').mockImplementation(() => {})` in a `beforeEach` / `afterEach` pair. ``` describe('ErrorBoundary') ✗ renders children when no error occurs ✗ catches an error thrown by a child and renders fallback UI instead of crashing ✗ fallback UI contains a user-friendly error message (e.g. "Something went wrong") ✗ fallback UI contains a "Try again" button ✗ clicking "Try again" unmounts/remounts children (resets error state) ✗ calls onError prop callback when an error is caught ``` **`client/src/App.test.jsx`** additions ``` describe('App with ErrorBoundary') ✗ renders Board inside an ErrorBoundary (ErrorBoundary component present in tree) ✗ if Board throws, ErrorBoundary fallback is shown not a blank screen ``` ### Implementation **`client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.jsx`** ```jsx import { Component } from 'react' export default class ErrorBoundary extends Component { constructor(props) { super(props) this.state = { hasError: false, error: null } } static getDerivedStateFromError(error) { return { hasError: true, error } } componentDidCatch(error, info) { this.props.onError?.(error, info) // In production this would go to an error reporting service console.error('[ErrorBoundary] Caught:', error, info.componentStack) } handleRetry = () => { this.setState({ hasError: false, error: null }) } render() { if (this.state.hasError) { if (this.props.fallback) return this.props.fallback return (

{this.state.error?.message ?? 'An unexpected error occurred.'}

) } return this.props.children } } ``` **`client/src/App.jsx`** changes ```jsx import ErrorBoundary from './components/ErrorBoundary.jsx' // Wrap Board:
``` **`client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx`** changes - Import `LoadingSpinner` from `'../LoadingSpinner.jsx'` - Replace: ```jsx if (loading) return
Loading…
``` With: ```jsx if (loading) return ``` --- ## Subtask 6 — Production CORS and Enhanced Health Check ### Files to create/modify - **MODIFY** `server/index.js` (enhanced CORS, enhanced `/health`, new `/ready`, `/live`, `/metrics`) - **MODIFY** `server/ws/broadcaster.js` (add `getWsStatus()` export) - **CREATE** `server/test/health.test.mjs` (comprehensive health check tests) - **MODIFY** `server/test/server.test.mjs` (fix existing health test that will break) ### Existing test that MUST be updated in `server/test/server.test.mjs` Line ~166: ```js it('returns { ok: true }', async () => { assert.deepStrictEqual(body, { ok: true }); // ← WILL BREAK with new fields }) ``` Change to check individual fields: ```js it('returns ok: true in body', async () => { assert.strictEqual(body.ok, true) }) ``` ### Tests to write first (`server/test/health.test.mjs`) ``` describe('GET /health — enhanced') ✗ returns HTTP 200 ✗ body.ok === true ✗ body.db is 'ok' when database is accessible (test uses in-memory DB via initDb(':memory:')) ✗ body.ws is 'ok' or 'not_initialized' (string) ✗ body.uptime is a positive number ✗ body.timestamp is a valid ISO 8601 string describe('GET /ready — readiness probe') ✗ returns 200 { ready: true } when DB is reachable ✗ Content-Type is application/json describe('GET /live — liveness probe') ✗ returns 200 { alive: true } unconditionally ✗ Content-Type is application/json describe('GET /metrics') ✗ returns 200 ✗ body.uptime is a positive number ✗ body.memory.heapUsed is a positive number ✗ body.memory.rss is a positive number ✗ body.timestamp is a valid ISO 8601 string describe('CORS — production with ALLOWED_ORIGINS') ✗ allowed origin receives Access-Control-Allow-Origin matching that origin ✗ disallowed origin receives no Access-Control-Allow-Origin header ✗ OPTIONS preflight for allowed origin returns 2xx ✗ when ALLOWED_ORIGINS not set in production, no ACAO header for any origin ``` ### Implementation **`server/ws/broadcaster.js`** — add one export: ```js export function getWsStatus() { return wss ? 'ok' : 'not_initialized' } ``` **`server/index.js` — CORS (position 3)** Replace current CORS middleware with: ```js const allowedOrigins = process.env.ALLOWED_ORIGINS ? process.env.ALLOWED_ORIGINS.split(',').map(s => s.trim()) : [] app.use(cors({ origin: isProduction ? (origin, cb) => { if (!origin || allowedOrigins.includes(origin)) cb(null, true) else cb(null, false) } : true, credentials: true, methods: ['GET', 'POST', 'PATCH', 'DELETE', 'OPTIONS'], allowedHeaders: ['Content-Type', 'Authorization'], })) ``` **`server/index.js` — enhanced `/health` (position 6)** Add imports: `import { getWsStatus } from './ws/broadcaster.js'` (already imports `initWs`) ```js app.get('/health', (_req, res) => { let dbStatus = 'ok' try { getDb().prepare('SELECT 1').get() } catch { dbStatus = 'error' } res.json({ ok: true, db: dbStatus, ws: getWsStatus(), uptime: process.uptime(), timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), }) }) ``` **`server/index.js` — `/ready` (position 7)** ```js app.get('/ready', (_req, res) => { try { getDb().prepare('SELECT 1').get() res.json({ ready: true }) } catch { res.status(503).json({ ready: false, reason: 'Database unavailable' }) } }) ``` **`server/index.js` — `/live` (position 8)** ```js app.get('/live', (_req, res) => res.json({ alive: true })) ``` **`server/index.js` — `/metrics` (position 9)** ```js app.get('/metrics', (_req, res) => { const mem = process.memoryUsage() res.json({ uptime: process.uptime(), memory: { rss: mem.rss, heapUsed: mem.heapUsed, heapTotal: mem.heapTotal, }, timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), }) }) ``` **Note on `getDb()` in `/health`**: When running tests, `initDb(':memory:')` is called in test setup. When `createApp()` is called in tests **without** calling `initDb` first, `getDb()` will throw or return null. The `/health` endpoint already handles this with the try/catch (`dbStatus = 'error'`). Tests that want `db: 'ok'` must call `initDb(':memory:')` before creating the app. --- ## Execution Order and Test Commands ### Server package.json — update test scripts After adding new test files, update the `test` script to include them: ```json "test": "node --test test/server.test.mjs test/db.test.mjs test/comments.test.mjs test/ws.test.mjs test/cards.test.mjs test/errorHandler.test.mjs test/logger.test.mjs test/security.test.mjs test/health.test.mjs" ``` Update `test:all` and `test:coverage` similarly. ### Server tests ```bash cd server && npm test # Individual new test files: node --test test/errorHandler.test.mjs node --test test/logger.test.mjs node --test test/security.test.mjs node --test test/health.test.mjs npm run test:coverage ``` ### Client tests ```bash cd client && npm test # Individual new test files: npx vitest run src/components/ErrorBoundary.test.jsx npx vitest run src/components/LoadingSpinner.test.jsx npx vitest run src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx npm run test:coverage ``` ### End-to-end verification 1. `npm run dev` (from root) — check coloured morgan logs appear in console 2. `curl http://localhost:3001/health` — verify `{ ok, db, ws, uptime, timestamp }` 3. `curl http://localhost:3001/ready` — verify `{ ready: true }` 4. `curl http://localhost:3001/live` — verify `{ alive: true }` 5. `curl http://localhost:3001/metrics` — verify `{ uptime, memory, timestamp }` 6. `curl http://localhost:3001/nonexistent` — verify `{ error: '...', code: 'NOT_FOUND' }` 7. `curl -H "Origin: http://evil.com" http://localhost:3001/api/cards` with `NODE_ENV=production` and no `ALLOWED_ORIGINS` set — verify no `access-control-allow-origin` header 8. Rapid-fire `for i in {1..101}; do curl -s http://localhost:3001/api/cards; done` — verify 429 with `Retry-After` 9. `npm run build` (from client/) — inspect `dist/assets/` for `vendor-react-*.js`, `vendor-dnd-*.js`, `vendor-blocknote-*.js` chunks --- ## Critical Files Summary | File | Action | |------|--------| | `server/index.js` | Modify — wire all middleware in correct order; enhanced health/ready/live/metrics/CORS; add `getDb` import | | `server/middleware/errorHandler.js` | Create — `ValidationError` class, `notFoundHandler`, `errorHandler` | | `server/middleware/logger.js` | Create — `createMorganMiddleware()` with rotating file support; `logger` object | | `server/middleware/security.js` | Create — `createHelmetMiddleware()`, `createApiRateLimiter()` | | `server/ws/broadcaster.js` | Modify — add `getWsStatus()` export | | `server/package.json` | Modify — add `morgan`, `rotating-file-stream`, `helmet`, `express-rate-limit`; update test scripts | | `server/test/errorHandler.test.mjs` | Create — TDD tests for error middleware | | `server/test/logger.test.mjs` | Create — TDD tests for logging | | `server/test/security.test.mjs` | Create — TDD tests for helmet + rate limiting + body size | | `server/test/health.test.mjs` | Create — TDD tests for enhanced health/ready/live/metrics/CORS | | `server/test/server.test.mjs` | Modify — fix `deepStrictEqual(body, { ok: true })` → `strictEqual(body.ok, true)` | | `client/vite.config.js` | Modify — add `build.rollupOptions.output.manualChunks` | | `client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.jsx` | Create — class-based error boundary with retry | | `client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.test.jsx` | Create — TDD tests for error boundary | | `client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.jsx` | Create — reusable loading spinner | | `client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.css` | Create — spinner animation styles | | `client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.test.jsx` | Create — TDD tests for spinner | | `client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx` | Modify — replace loading div with `` | | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx` | Modify — lazy-load BlockEditor with `React.lazy` + `` | | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx` | Modify — wrap all renders in ``; add lazy mock test | | `client/src/App.jsx` | Modify — wrap `` in `` | | `client/src/App.test.jsx` | Modify — add ErrorBoundary integration tests |

# Task 24: Production Optimizations and Error Handling ## Context The Kanban board Express + React app needs production-grade hardening. Currently: - The error handler is a 5-line inline function in `server/index.js` with no error typing or logging - The `/health` endpoint returns `{ ok: true }` with no database connectivity or WS status test - CORS in production is `origin: false` (no configurable allow-list, no methods/headers config) - No request logging (morgan), no rate limiting, no security headers (helmet) - Vite config has no manual chunk splitting or lazy loading - React has no `ErrorBoundary` component; loading states exist only at the board level All work follows TDD: **write failing tests first, then implement**. --- ## Final Middleware Order in `server/index.js` All subtasks modify `server/index.js`. The **final** middleware order must be: ``` 1. morgan (request logging) — createMorganMiddleware() 2. helmet (security headers) — createHelmetMiddleware() 3. cors (CORS headers) — cors({ origin, credentials, methods, allowedHeaders }) 4. express.json({ limit: '1mb' }) — body parsing + size limit 5. /api routes with rate limiter — app.use('/api', createApiRateLimiter(), commentsRouter, cardsRouter) 6. /health route — no rate limiter 7. /ready route — no rate limiter 8. /live route — no rate limiter 9. /metrics route — no rate limiter 10. static files (production only) 11. notFoundHandler — from middleware/errorHandler.js 12. errorHandler — from middleware/errorHandler.js (4-arg) ``` --- ## Subtask 1 — Comprehensive Error Handling Middleware ### Files to create/modify - **CREATE** `server/middleware/errorHandler.js` - **CREATE** `server/test/errorHandler.test.mjs` - **MODIFY** `server/index.js` (replace inline handlers; wire `notFoundHandler` + `errorHandler`) ### Tests to write first (`server/test/errorHandler.test.mjs`) ``` describe('errorHandler middleware') ✗ returns { error, code } shape on any generic Error ✗ uses err.status / err.statusCode for HTTP status (default 500) ✗ returns 400 + code 'VALIDATION_ERROR' for ValidationError ✗ returns 404 + code 'NOT_FOUND' for NotFoundError ✗ returns 500 + code 'DATABASE_ERROR' for DatabaseError ✗ returns 500 + code 'DATABASE_ERROR' for ForeignKeyError ✗ in production, response body does NOT include a stack field ✗ code is 'INTERNAL_ERROR' for generic Error with no special type describe('404 handler') ✗ unmatched GET returns 404 JSON { error: string, code: 'NOT_FOUND' } describe('/dev/error route with new middleware') ✗ default returns 500 with body.code === 'INTERNAL_ERROR' ✗ ?status=404 returns 404 with body.code === 'INTERNAL_ERROR' (generic err with err.status) ``` ### Implementation **`server/middleware/errorHandler.js`** Define `ValidationError` here (exported for route handlers to use): ```js export class ValidationError extends Error {} ``` Import `NotFoundError`, `DatabaseError`, `ForeignKeyError` from `../db/queries.js`. Export `notFoundHandler(req, res, next)`: - Creates `new NotFoundError('Not Found')` and calls `next(err)` Export `errorHandler(err, req, res, next)` (4-argument): ``` if (err instanceof ValidationError) → 400, code 'VALIDATION_ERROR' if (err instanceof NotFoundError) → 404, code 'NOT_FOUND' if (err instanceof DatabaseError) → 500, code 'DATABASE_ERROR' (covers ForeignKeyError) otherwise → err.status || err.statusCode || 500, code 'INTERNAL_ERROR' ``` Response shape always: `{ error: string, code: string }` — no stack trace in body ever. Side effect: `console.error(err.stack)` in all environments (server-side log, never in response body). **`server/index.js` changes** - Import: `import { notFoundHandler, errorHandler, ValidationError } from './middleware/errorHandler.js'` - Import: also add `getDb` to the existing import from `./db/queries.js`: `import { initDb, getDb } from './db/queries.js'` - Remove the inline `app.use((_req, res) => ...)` 404 handler - Remove the inline `app.use((err, _req, res, _next) => ...)` error handler - Add `app.use(notFoundHandler)` and `app.use(errorHandler)` at positions 11 and 12 per the middleware order above - Export `ValidationError` from `server/index.js` so routes can import from one place (re-export) **`server/test/server.test.mjs` changes (must update existing assertions)** - The existing `it('404 response body has "error" field')` test uses `assert.ok(body.error)` — this still passes (no change needed there) - The existing `it('response body has "error" string field')` uses `assert.strictEqual(typeof body.error, 'string')` — still passes - **No breaking changes** to existing server.test.mjs assertions from this subtask alone; `{ error, code }` is a superset of `{ error }` --- ## Subtask 2 — Request Logging with Morgan ### Files to create/modify - **CREATE** `server/middleware/logger.js` - **CREATE** `server/test/logger.test.mjs` - **MODIFY** `server/index.js` (add morgan as first middleware) - **MODIFY** `server/package.json` (add `morgan`, `rotating-file-stream`) ### Install ``` npm install morgan rotating-file-stream --prefix server ``` ### Tests to write first (`server/test/logger.test.mjs`) ``` describe('morgan dependency') ✗ package.json lists morgan as a dependency ✗ morgan can be imported as ES module describe('createMorganMiddleware()') ✗ returns a function (valid Express middleware) ✗ in development, GET /health produces a log line to stdout (capture stdout, check non-empty) ✗ in production, a log line is emitted in combined format describe('logger utility') ✗ logger.info(msg) writes a line containing the message to stdout ✗ logger.error(msg, err) writes a line containing the message to stderr ✗ logger.warn(msg) writes a line containing the message to stdout ✗ each log line contains a UTC timestamp ``` ### Implementation **`server/middleware/logger.js`** ```js import morgan from 'morgan' import { createWriteStream } from 'node:fs' import { mkdirSync } from 'node:fs' import { join, dirname } from 'node:path' import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url' import { createStream } from 'rotating-file-stream' const __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)) export function createMorganMiddleware() { const isProduction = process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production' if (!isProduction) { return morgan('dev') // coloured output to stdout } // Production: combined format to stdout AND rotating log file const logDir = join(__dirname, '..', 'logs') mkdirSync(logDir, { recursive: true }) const rotatingStream = createStream('access.log', { interval: '1d', // rotate daily path: logDir, size: '10M', // also rotate when file exceeds 10MB }) // Write to both stdout and the rotating file return morgan('combined', { stream: { write(msg) { process.stdout.write(msg) rotatingStream.write(msg) } } }) } // Structured app-level logger const timestamp = () => new Date().toISOString() export const logger = { info: (msg) => process.stdout.write(`[${timestamp()}] INFO ${msg}\n`), warn: (msg) => process.stdout.write(`[${timestamp()}] WARN ${msg}\n`), error: (msg, err) => process.stderr.write(`[${timestamp()}] ERROR ${msg}${err ? ': ' + err.message : ''}\n`), } ``` **`server/index.js` changes** - Import `createMorganMiddleware` from `./middleware/logger.js` - Add `app.use(createMorganMiddleware())` as the **first** `app.use()` call (position 1 per middleware order) --- ## Subtask 3 — Rate Limiting and Security Headers ### Files to create/modify - **CREATE** `server/middleware/security.js` - **CREATE** `server/test/security.test.mjs` - **MODIFY** `server/index.js` (add helmet + rate limiting; add body size limit) - **MODIFY** `server/package.json` (add `helmet`, `express-rate-limit`) ### Install ``` npm install helmet express-rate-limit --prefix server ``` ### Tests to write first (`server/test/security.test.mjs`) ``` describe('security dependencies') ✗ helmet can be imported ✗ express-rate-limit can be imported ✗ package.json lists helmet ✗ package.json lists express-rate-limit describe('helmet security headers') ✗ GET /health response includes X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff ✗ GET /health response includes X-Frame-Options header ✗ GET /health response does NOT include X-Powered-By describe('API rate limiting') ✗ after exceeding the limit, GET /api/cards returns HTTP 429 ✗ 429 response includes Retry-After header ✗ 429 response body is JSON { error: string, code: 'RATE_LIMITED' } ✗ GET /health is NOT blocked after rate limit triggered on /api describe('request body size limit') ✗ POST /dev/echo with body > 1mb returns 413 ``` ### Implementation **`server/middleware/security.js`** ```js import helmet from 'helmet' import rateLimit from 'express-rate-limit' export function createHelmetMiddleware() { return helmet() // enables all default protections; removes X-Powered-By } export function createApiRateLimiter(options = {}) { return rateLimit({ windowMs: options.windowMs ?? 15 * 60 * 1000, // 15 minutes default max: options.max ?? 100, standardHeaders: true, legacyHeaders: false, // Memory store (default) — sufficient for single-instance; Redis would be // needed for multi-instance deployments but is out of scope for this project handler(req, res) { res.status(429).json({ error: 'Too many requests', code: 'RATE_LIMITED' }) }, }) } ``` **`server/index.js` changes** - Import `createHelmetMiddleware`, `createApiRateLimiter` from `./middleware/security.js` - Position 2: `app.use(createHelmetMiddleware())` - Position 4: change `app.use(express.json())` to `app.use(express.json({ limit: '1mb' }))` - Position 5: `app.use('/api', createApiRateLimiter(), commentsRouter, cardsRouter)` - Remove the prior `app.use('/api', commentsRouter)` and `app.use('/api', cardsRouter)` lines - The rate limiter is scoped to `/api` only; `/health`, `/ready`, `/live`, `/metrics` are exempt - No `createStrictRateLimiter` needed — a single limiter for all `/api` routes is sufficient --- ## Subtask 4 — Vite Bundle Optimization (Code Splitting) ### Files to create/modify - **MODIFY** `client/vite.config.js` (add `build.rollupOptions.output.manualChunks`) - **MODIFY** `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx` (lazy-load BlockEditor) - **CREATE** `client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.jsx` + `LoadingSpinner.css` - **MODIFY** `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx` (wrap renders in ``) - **CREATE** `client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.test.jsx` ### Tests to write first **`client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.test.jsx`** (new, write first) ``` describe('LoadingSpinner') ✗ renders an element with role="status" ✗ has aria-label="Loading" ✗ renders an element with class "loading-spinner" ``` **`client/src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx`** (update existing — critical) `CardModal.jsx` will use `React.lazy` to import `BlockEditor`. React requires every lazy component to be inside a `` boundary, even in tests. Without it, React throws. ``` With: ```jsx if (loading) return ``` --- ## Subtask 6 — Production CORS and Enhanced Health Check ### Files to create/modify - **MODIFY** `server/index.js` (enhanced CORS, enhanced `/health`, new `/ready`, `/live`, `/metrics`) - **MODIFY** `server/ws/broadcaster.js` (add `getWsStatus()` export) - **CREATE** `server/test/health.test.mjs` (comprehensive health check tests) - **MODIFY** `server/test/server.test.mjs` (fix existing health test that will break) ### Existing test that MUST be updated in `server/test/server.test.mjs` Line ~166: ```js it('returns { ok: true }', async () => { assert.deepStrictEqual(body, { ok: true }); // ← WILL BREAK with new fields }) ``` Change to check individual fields: ```js it('returns ok: true in body', async () => { assert.strictEqual(body.ok, true) }) ``` ### Tests to write first (`server/test/health.test.mjs`) ``` describe('GET /health — enhanced') ✗ returns HTTP 200 ✗ body.ok === true ✗ body.db is 'ok' when database is accessible (test uses in-memory DB via initDb(':memory:')) ✗ body.ws is 'ok' or 'not_initialized' (string) ✗ body.uptime is a positive number ✗ body.timestamp is a valid ISO 8601 string describe('GET /ready — readiness probe') ✗ returns 200 { ready: true } when DB is reachable ✗ Content-Type is application/json describe('GET /live — liveness probe') ✗ returns 200 { alive: true } unconditionally ✗ Content-Type is application/json describe('GET /metrics') ✗ returns 200 ✗ body.uptime is a positive number ✗ body.memory.heapUsed is a positive number ✗ body.memory.rss is a positive number ✗ body.timestamp is a valid ISO 8601 string describe('CORS — production with ALLOWED_ORIGINS') ✗ allowed origin receives Access-Control-Allow-Origin matching that origin ✗ disallowed origin receives no Access-Control-Allow-Origin header ✗ OPTIONS preflight for allowed origin returns 2xx ✗ when ALLOWED_ORIGINS not set in production, no ACAO header for any origin ``` ### Implementation **`server/ws/broadcaster.js`** — add one export: ```js export function getWsStatus() { return wss ? 'ok' : 'not_initialized' } ``` **`server/index.js` — CORS (position 3)** Replace current CORS middleware with: ```js const allowedOrigins = process.env.ALLOWED_ORIGINS ? process.env.ALLOWED_ORIGINS.split(',').map(s => s.trim()) : [] app.use(cors({ origin: isProduction ? (origin, cb) => { if (!origin || allowedOrigins.includes(origin)) cb(null, true) else cb(null, false) } : true, credentials: true, methods: ['GET', 'POST', 'PATCH', 'DELETE', 'OPTIONS'], allowedHeaders: ['Content-Type', 'Authorization'], })) ``` **`server/index.js` — enhanced `/health` (position 6)** Add imports: `import { getWsStatus } from './ws/broadcaster.js'` (already imports `initWs`) ```js app.get('/health', (_req, res) => { let dbStatus = 'ok' try { getDb().prepare('SELECT 1').get() } catch { dbStatus = 'error' } res.json({ ok: true, db: dbStatus, ws: getWsStatus(), uptime: process.uptime(), timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), }) }) ``` **`server/index.js` — `/ready` (position 7)** ```js app.get('/ready', (_req, res) => { try { getDb().prepare('SELECT 1').get() res.json({ ready: true }) } catch { res.status(503).json({ ready: false, reason: 'Database unavailable' }) } }) ``` **`server/index.js` — `/live` (position 8)** ```js app.get('/live', (_req, res) => res.json({ alive: true })) ``` **`server/index.js` — `/metrics` (position 9)** ```js app.get('/metrics', (_req, res) => { const mem = process.memoryUsage() res.json({ uptime: process.uptime(), memory: { rss: mem.rss, heapUsed: mem.heapUsed, heapTotal: mem.heapTotal, }, timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), }) }) ``` **Note on `getDb()` in `/health`**: When running tests, `initDb(':memory:')` is called in test setup. When `createApp()` is called in tests **without** calling `initDb` first, `getDb()` will throw or return null. The `/health` endpoint already handles this with the try/catch (`dbStatus = 'error'`). Tests that want `db: 'ok'` must call `initDb(':memory:')` before creating the app. --- ## Execution Order and Test Commands ### Server package.json — update test scripts After adding new test files, update the `test` script to include them: ```json "test": "node --test test/server.test.mjs test/db.test.mjs test/comments.test.mjs test/ws.test.mjs test/cards.test.mjs test/errorHandler.test.mjs test/logger.test.mjs test/security.test.mjs test/health.test.mjs" ``` Update `test:all` and `test:coverage` similarly. ### Server tests ```bash cd server && npm test # Individual new test files: node --test test/errorHandler.test.mjs node --test test/logger.test.mjs node --test test/security.test.mjs node --test test/health.test.mjs npm run test:coverage ``` ### Client tests ```bash cd client && npm test # Individual new test files: npx vitest run src/components/ErrorBoundary.test.jsx npx vitest run src/components/LoadingSpinner.test.jsx npx vitest run src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx npm run test:coverage ``` ### End-to-end verification 1. `npm run dev` (from root) — check coloured morgan logs appear in console 2. `curl http://localhost:3001/health` — verify `{ ok, db, ws, uptime, timestamp }` 3. `curl http://localhost:3001/ready` — verify `{ ready: true }` 4. `curl http://localhost:3001/live` — verify `{ alive: true }` 5. `curl http://localhost:3001/metrics` — verify `{ uptime, memory, timestamp }` 6. `curl http://localhost:3001/nonexistent` — verify `{ error: '...', code: 'NOT_FOUND' }` 7. `curl -H "Origin: http://evil.com" http://localhost:3001/api/cards` with `NODE_ENV=production` and no `ALLOWED_ORIGINS` set — verify no `access-control-allow-origin` header 8.
