# Task 24: Production Optimizations and Error Handling ## Context The Kanban board Express + React app needs production-grade hardening. Currently: - The error handler is a 5-line inline function in `server/index.js` with no error typing or logging - The `/health` endpoint returns `{ ok: true }` with no database connectivity or WS status test - CORS in production is `origin: false` (no configurable allow-list, no methods/headers config) - No request logging (morgan), no rate limiting, no security headers (helmet) - Vite config has no manual chunk splitting or lazy loading - React has no `ErrorBoundary` component; loading states exist only at the board level All work follows TDD: **write failing tests first, then implement**. --- ## Final Middleware Order in `server/index.js` All subtasks modify `server/index.js`. The **final** middleware order must be: ``` 1. morgan (request logging) — createMorganMiddleware() 2. helmet (security headers) — createHelmetMiddleware() 3. cors (CORS headers) — cors({ origin, credentials, methods, allowedHeaders }) 4. express.json({ limit: '1mb' }) — body parsing + size limit 5. /api routes with rate limiter — app.use('/api', createApiRateLimiter(), commentsRouter, cardsRouter) 6. /health route — no rate limiter 7. /ready route — no rate limiter 8. /live route — no rate limiter 9. /metrics route — no rate limiter 10. static files (production only) 11. notFoundHandler — from middleware/errorHandler.js 12. errorHandler — from middleware/errorHandler.js (4-arg) ``` --- ## Subtask 1 — Comprehensive Error Handling Middleware ### Files to create/modify - **CREATE** `server/middleware/errorHandler.js` - **CREATE** `server/test/errorHandler.test.mjs` - **MODIFY** `server/index.js` (replace inline handlers; wire `notFoundHandler` + `errorHandler`) ### Tests to write first (`server/test/errorHandler.test.mjs`) ``` describe('errorHandler middleware') ✗ returns { error, code } shape on any generic Error ✗ uses err.status / err.statusCode for HTTP status (default 500) ✗ returns 400 + code 'VALIDATION_ERROR' for ValidationError ✗ returns 404 + code 'NOT_FOUND' for NotFoundError ✗ returns 500 + code 'DATABASE_ERROR' for DatabaseError ✗ returns 500 + code 'DATABASE_ERROR' for ForeignKeyError ✗ in production, response body does NOT include a stack field ✗ code is 'INTERNAL_ERROR' for generic Error with no special type describe('404 handler') ✗ unmatched GET returns 404 JSON { error: string, code: 'NOT_FOUND' } describe('/dev/error route with new middleware') ✗ default returns 500 with body.code === 'INTERNAL_ERROR' ✗ ?status=404 returns 404 with body.code === 'INTERNAL_ERROR' (generic err with err.status) ``` ### Implementation **`server/middleware/errorHandler.js`** Define `ValidationError` here (exported for route handlers to use): ```js export class ValidationError extends Error {} ``` Import `NotFoundError`, `DatabaseError`, `ForeignKeyError` from `../db/queries.js`. Export `notFoundHandler(req, res, next)`: - Creates `new NotFoundError('Not Found')` and calls `next(err)` Export `errorHandler(err, req, res, next)` (4-argument): ``` if (err instanceof ValidationError) → 400, code 'VALIDATION_ERROR' if (err instanceof NotFoundError) → 404, code 'NOT_FOUND' if (err instanceof DatabaseError) → 500, code 'DATABASE_ERROR' (covers ForeignKeyError) otherwise → err.status || err.statusCode || 500, code 'INTERNAL_ERROR' ``` Response shape always: `{ error: string, code: string }` — no stack trace in body ever. Side effect: `console.error(err.stack)` in all environments (server-side log, never in response body). **`server/index.js` changes** - Import: `import { notFoundHandler, errorHandler, ValidationError } from './middleware/errorHandler.js'` - Import: also add `getDb` to the existing import from `./db/queries.js`: `import { initDb, getDb } from './db/queries.js'` - Remove the inline `app.use((_req, res) => ...)` 404 handler - Remove the inline `app.use((err, _req, res, _next) => ...)` error handler - Add `app.use(notFoundHandler)` and `app.use(errorHandler)` at positions 11 and 12 per the middleware order above - Export `ValidationError` from `server/index.js` so routes can import from one place (re-export) **`server/test/server.test.mjs` changes (must update existing assertions)** - The existing `it('404 response body has "error" field')` test uses `assert.ok(body.error)` — this still passes (no change needed there) - The existing `it('response body has "error" string field')` uses `assert.strictEqual(typeof body.error, 'string')` — still passes - **No breaking changes** to existing server.test.mjs assertions from this subtask alone; `{ error, code }` is a superset of `{ error }` --- ## Subtask 2 — Request Logging with Morgan ### Files to create/modify - **CREATE** `server/middleware/logger.js` - **CREATE** `server/test/logger.test.mjs` - **MODIFY** `server/index.js` (add morgan as first middleware) - **MODIFY** `server/package.json` (add `morgan`, `rotating-file-stream`) ### Install ``` npm install morgan rotating-file-stream --prefix server ``` ### Tests to write first (`server/test/logger.test.mjs`) ``` describe('morgan dependency') ✗ package.json lists morgan as a dependency ✗ morgan can be imported as ES module describe('createMorganMiddleware()') ✗ returns a function (valid Express middleware) ✗ in development, GET /health produces a log line to stdout (capture stdout, check non-empty) ✗ in production, a log line is emitted in combined format describe('logger utility') ✗ logger.info(msg) writes a line containing the message to stdout ✗ logger.error(msg, err) writes a line containing the message to stderr ✗ logger.warn(msg) writes a line containing the message to stdout ✗ each log line contains a UTC timestamp ``` ### Implementation **`server/middleware/logger.js`** ```js import morgan from 'morgan' import { createWriteStream } from 'node:fs' import { mkdirSync } from 'node:fs' import { join, dirname } from 'node:path' import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url' import { createStream } from 'rotating-file-stream' const __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)) export function createMorganMiddleware() { const isProduction = process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production' if (!isProduction) { return morgan('dev') // coloured output to stdout } // Production: combined format to stdout AND rotating log file const logDir = join(__dirname, '..', 'logs') mkdirSync(logDir, { recursive: true }) const rotatingStream = createStream('access.log', { interval: '1d', // rotate daily path: logDir, size: '10M', // also rotate when file exceeds 10MB }) // Write to both stdout and the rotating file return morgan('combined', { stream: { write(msg) { process.stdout.write(msg) rotatingStream.write(msg) } } }) } // Structured app-level logger const timestamp = () => new Date().toISOString() export const logger = { info: (msg) => process.stdout.write(`[${timestamp()}] INFO ${msg}\n`), warn: (msg) => process.stdout.write(`[${timestamp()}] WARN ${msg}\n`), error: (msg, err) => process.stderr.write(`[${timestamp()}] ERROR ${msg}${err ? ': ' + err.message : ''}\n`), } ``` **`server/index.js` changes** - Import `createMorganMiddleware` from `./middleware/logger.js` - Add `app.use(createMorganMiddleware())` as the **first** `app.use()` call (position 1 per middleware order) --- ## Subtask 3 — Rate Limiting and Security Headers ### Files to create/modify - **CREATE** `server/middleware/security.js` - **CREATE** `server/test/security.test.mjs` - **MODIFY** `server/index.js` (add helmet + rate limiting; add body size limit) - **MODIFY** `server/package.json` (add `helmet`, `express-rate-limit`) ### Install ``` npm install helmet express-rate-limit --prefix server ``` ### Tests to write first (`server/test/security.test.mjs`) ``` describe('security dependencies') ✗ helmet can be imported ✗ express-rate-limit can be imported ✗ package.json lists helmet ✗ package.json lists express-rate-limit describe('helmet security headers') ✗ GET /health response includes X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff ✗ GET /health response includes X-Frame-Options header ✗ GET /health response does NOT include X-Powered-By describe('API rate limiting') ✗ after exceeding the limit, GET /api/cards returns HTTP 429 ✗ 429 response includes Retry-After header ✗ 429 response body is JSON { error: string, code: 'RATE_LIMITED' } ✗ GET /health is NOT blocked after rate limit triggered on /api describe('request body size limit') ✗ POST /dev/echo with body > 1mb returns 413 ``` ### Implementation **`server/middleware/security.js`** ```js import helmet from 'helmet' import rateLimit from 'express-rate-limit' export function createHelmetMiddleware() { return helmet() // enables all default protections; removes X-Powered-By } export function createApiRateLimiter(options = {}) { return rateLimit({ windowMs: options.windowMs ?? 15 * 60 * 1000, // 15 minutes default max: options.max ?? 100, standardHeaders: true, legacyHeaders: false, // Memory store (default) — sufficient for single-instance; Redis would be // needed for multi-instance deployments but is out of scope for this project handler(req, res) { res.status(429).json({ error: 'Too many requests', code: 'RATE_LIMITED' }) }, }) } ``` **`server/index.js` changes** - Import `createHelmetMiddleware`, `createApiRateLimiter` from `./middleware/security.js` - Position 2: `app.use(createHelmetMiddleware())` - Position 4: change `app.use(express.json())` to `app.use(express.json({ limit: '1mb' }))` - Position 5: `app.use('/api', createApiRateLimiter(), commentsRouter, cardsRouter)` - Remove the prior `app.use('/api', commentsRouter)` and `app.use('/api', cardsRouter)` lines - The rate limiter is scoped to `/api` only; `/health`, `/ready`, `/live`, `/metrics` are exempt - No `createStrictRateLimiter` needed — a single limiter for all `/api` routes is sufficient --- ## Subtask 4 — Vite Bundle Optimization (Code Splitting) ### Files to create/modify - **MODIFY** `client/vite.config.js` (add `build.rollupOptions.output.manualChunks`) - **MODIFY** `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx` (lazy-load BlockEditor) - **CREATE** `client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.jsx` + `LoadingSpinner.css` - **MODIFY** `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx` (wrap renders in ``) - **CREATE** `client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.test.jsx` ### Tests to write first **`client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.test.jsx`** (new, write first) ``` describe('LoadingSpinner') ✗ renders an element with role="status" ✗ has aria-label="Loading" ✗ renders an element with class "loading-spinner" ``` **`client/src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx`** (update existing — critical) `CardModal.jsx` will use `React.lazy` to import `BlockEditor`. React requires every lazy component to be inside a `` boundary, even in tests. Without it, React throws. The existing `vi.mock('../CardModal/BlockEditor.jsx', ...)` mock resolves synchronously, so the Suspense fallback will NOT visually appear in tests, but the boundary must exist. **Update every `render(...)` call** in the file to wrap CardModal in Suspense: ```jsx import { Suspense } from 'react' // Change: render() // To: render(Loading}>) ``` Add one new test: ``` describe('CardModal with lazy BlockEditor') ✗ block editor content is rendered (lazy mock resolves synchronously) ``` ### Implementation **`client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.jsx`** ```jsx import './LoadingSpinner.css' export default function LoadingSpinner() { return (
) } ``` **`client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.css`** ```css .loading-spinner { display: flex; justify-content: center; align-items: center; padding: 2rem; } .spinner-ring { display: block; width: 2rem; height: 2rem; border: 3px solid #e2e8f0; border-top-color: #4f46e5; border-radius: 50%; animation: spin 0.75s linear infinite; } @keyframes spin { to { transform: rotate(360deg); } } ``` **`client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx`** changes ```jsx import React, { Suspense, useEffect, useState, useRef } from 'react' import LoadingSpinner from '../LoadingSpinner.jsx' // Remove: import BlockEditor from '../CardModal/BlockEditor.jsx' // Add: const BlockEditor = React.lazy(() => import('../CardModal/BlockEditor.jsx')) // Wrap the BlockEditor usage with: }> ``` **`client/vite.config.js`** changes — add a `build` key: ```js build: { rollupOptions: { output: { manualChunks: { 'vendor-react': ['react', 'react-dom'], 'vendor-dnd': ['@dnd-kit/core', '@dnd-kit/sortable', '@dnd-kit/utilities'], 'vendor-blocknote': ['@blocknote/core', '@blocknote/react'], }, }, }, }, ``` --- ## Subtask 5 — React Error Boundaries and Loading States ### Files to create/modify - **CREATE** `client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.jsx` - **CREATE** `client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.test.jsx` - **MODIFY** `client/src/App.jsx` (wrap Board in ErrorBoundary) - **MODIFY** `client/src/App.test.jsx` (add ErrorBoundary integration tests) - **MODIFY** `client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx` (use LoadingSpinner for loading state) ### Tests to write first **`client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.test.jsx`** (new) React Testing Library emits `console.error` when a component throws inside an error boundary during tests. Suppress it with `vi.spyOn(console, 'error').mockImplementation(() => {})` in a `beforeEach` / `afterEach` pair. ``` describe('ErrorBoundary') ✗ renders children when no error occurs ✗ catches an error thrown by a child and renders fallback UI instead of crashing ✗ fallback UI contains a user-friendly error message (e.g. "Something went wrong") ✗ fallback UI contains a "Try again" button ✗ clicking "Try again" unmounts/remounts children (resets error state) ✗ calls onError prop callback when an error is caught ``` **`client/src/App.test.jsx`** additions ``` describe('App with ErrorBoundary') ✗ renders Board inside an ErrorBoundary (ErrorBoundary component present in tree) ✗ if Board throws, ErrorBoundary fallback is shown not a blank screen ``` ### Implementation **`client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.jsx`** ```jsx import { Component } from 'react' export default class ErrorBoundary extends Component { constructor(props) { super(props) this.state = { hasError: false, error: null } } static getDerivedStateFromError(error) { return { hasError: true, error } } componentDidCatch(error, info) { this.props.onError?.(error, info) // In production this would go to an error reporting service console.error('[ErrorBoundary] Caught:', error, info.componentStack) } handleRetry = () => { this.setState({ hasError: false, error: null }) } render() { if (this.state.hasError) { if (this.props.fallback) return this.props.fallback return (

) } return this.props.children } } ``` **`client/src/App.jsx`** changes ```jsx import ErrorBoundary from './components/ErrorBoundary.jsx' // Wrap Board:
``` **`client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx`** changes - Import `LoadingSpinner` from `'../LoadingSpinner.jsx'` - Replace: ```jsx if (loading) return
Loading…
``` With: ```jsx if (loading) return ``` --- ## Subtask 6 — Production CORS and Enhanced Health Check ### Files to create/modify - **MODIFY** `server/index.js` (enhanced CORS, enhanced `/health`, new `/ready`, `/live`, `/metrics`) - **MODIFY** `server/ws/broadcaster.js` (add `getWsStatus()` export) - **CREATE** `server/test/health.test.mjs` (comprehensive health check tests) - **MODIFY** `server/test/server.test.mjs` (fix existing health test that will break) ### Existing test that MUST be updated in `server/test/server.test.mjs` Line ~166: ```js it('returns { ok: true }', async () => { assert.deepStrictEqual(body, { ok: true }); // ← WILL BREAK with new fields }) ``` Change to check individual fields: ```js it('returns ok: true in body', async () => { assert.strictEqual(body.ok, true) }) ``` ### Tests to write first (`server/test/health.test.mjs`) ``` describe('GET /health — enhanced') ✗ returns HTTP 200 ✗ body.ok === true ✗ body.db is 'ok' when database is accessible (test uses in-memory DB via initDb(':memory:')) ✗ body.ws is 'ok' or 'not_initialized' (string) ✗ body.uptime is a positive number ✗ body.timestamp is a valid ISO 8601 string describe('GET /ready — readiness probe') ✗ returns 200 { ready: true } when DB is reachable ✗ Content-Type is application/json describe('GET /live — liveness probe') ✗ returns 200 { alive: true } unconditionally ✗ Content-Type is application/json describe('GET /metrics') ✗ returns 200 ✗ body.uptime is a positive number ✗ body.memory.heapUsed is a positive number ✗ body.memory.rss is a positive number ✗ body.timestamp is a valid ISO 8601 string describe('CORS — production with ALLOWED_ORIGINS') ✗ allowed origin receives Access-Control-Allow-Origin matching that origin ✗ disallowed origin receives no Access-Control-Allow-Origin header ✗ OPTIONS preflight for allowed origin returns 2xx ✗ when ALLOWED_ORIGINS not set in production, no ACAO header for any origin ``` ### Implementation **`server/ws/broadcaster.js`** — add one export: ```js export function getWsStatus() { return wss ? 'ok' : 'not_initialized' } ``` **`server/index.js` — CORS (position 3)** Replace current CORS middleware with: ```js const allowedOrigins = process.env.ALLOWED_ORIGINS ? process.env.ALLOWED_ORIGINS.split(',').map(s => s.trim()) : [] app.use(cors({ origin: isProduction ? (origin, cb) => { if (!origin || allowedOrigins.includes(origin)) cb(null, true) else cb(null, false) } : true, credentials: true, methods: ['GET', 'POST', 'PATCH', 'DELETE', 'OPTIONS'], allowedHeaders: ['Content-Type', 'Authorization'], })) ``` **`server/index.js` — enhanced `/health` (position 6)** Add imports: `import { getWsStatus } from './ws/broadcaster.js'` (already imports `initWs`) ```js app.get('/health', (_req, res) => { let dbStatus = 'ok' try { getDb().prepare('SELECT 1').get() } catch { dbStatus = 'error' } res.json({ ok: true, db: dbStatus, ws: getWsStatus(), uptime: process.uptime(), timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), }) }) ``` **`server/index.js` — `/ready` (position 7)** ```js app.get('/ready', (_req, res) => { try { getDb().prepare('SELECT 1').get() res.json({ ready: true }) } catch { res.status(503).json({ ready: false, reason: 'Database unavailable' }) } }) ``` **`server/index.js` — `/live` (position 8)** ```js app.get('/live', (_req, res) => res.json({ alive: true })) ``` **`server/index.js` — `/metrics` (position 9)** ```js app.get('/metrics', (_req, res) => { const mem = process.memoryUsage() res.json({ uptime: process.uptime(), memory: { rss: mem.rss, heapUsed: mem.heapUsed, heapTotal: mem.heapTotal, }, timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), }) }) ``` **Note on `getDb()` in `/health`**: When running tests, `initDb(':memory:')` is called in test setup. When `createApp()` is called in tests **without** calling `initDb` first, `getDb()` will throw or return null. The `/health` endpoint already handles this with the try/catch (`dbStatus = 'error'`). Tests that want `db: 'ok'` must call `initDb(':memory:')` before creating the app. --- ## Execution Order and Test Commands ### Server package.json — update test scripts After adding new test files, update the `test` script to include them: ```json "test": "node --test test/server.test.mjs test/db.test.mjs test/comments.test.mjs test/ws.test.mjs test/cards.test.mjs test/errorHandler.test.mjs test/logger.test.mjs test/security.test.mjs test/health.test.mjs" ``` Update `test:all` and `test:coverage` similarly. ### Server tests ```bash cd server && npm test # Individual new test files: node --test test/errorHandler.test.mjs node --test test/logger.test.mjs node --test test/security.test.mjs node --test test/health.test.mjs npm run test:coverage ``` ### Client tests ```bash cd client && npm test # Individual new test files: npx vitest run src/components/ErrorBoundary.test.jsx npx vitest run src/components/LoadingSpinner.test.jsx npx vitest run src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx npm run test:coverage ``` ### End-to-end verification 1. `npm run dev` (from root) — check coloured morgan logs appear in console 2. `curl http://localhost:3001/health` — verify `{ ok, db, ws, uptime, timestamp }` 3. `curl http://localhost:3001/ready` — verify `{ ready: true }` 4. `curl http://localhost:3001/live` — verify `{ alive: true }` 5. `curl http://localhost:3001/metrics` — verify `{ uptime, memory, timestamp }` 6. `curl http://localhost:3001/nonexistent` — verify `{ error: '...', code: 'NOT_FOUND' }` 7. `curl -H "Origin: http://evil.com" http://localhost:3001/api/cards` with `NODE_ENV=production` and no `ALLOWED_ORIGINS` set — verify no `access-control-allow-origin` header 8. Rapid-fire `for i in {1..101}; do curl -s http://localhost:3001/api/cards; done` — verify 429 with `Retry-After` 9. `npm run build` (from client/) — inspect `dist/assets/` for `vendor-react-*.js`, `vendor-dnd-*.js`, `vendor-blocknote-*.js` chunks --- ## Critical Files Summary | File | Action | |------|--------| | `server/index.js` | Modify — wire all middleware in correct order; enhanced health/ready/live/metrics/CORS; add `getDb` import | | `server/middleware/errorHandler.js` | Create — `ValidationError` class, `notFoundHandler`, `errorHandler` | | `server/middleware/logger.js` | Create — `createMorganMiddleware()` with rotating file support; `logger` object | | `server/middleware/security.js` | Create — `createHelmetMiddleware()`, `createApiRateLimiter()` | | `server/ws/broadcaster.js` | Modify — add `getWsStatus()` export | | `server/package.json` | Modify — add `morgan`, `rotating-file-stream`, `helmet`, `express-rate-limit`; update test scripts | | `server/test/errorHandler.test.mjs` | Create — TDD tests for error middleware | | `server/test/logger.test.mjs` | Create — TDD tests for logging | | `server/test/security.test.mjs` | Create — TDD tests for helmet + rate limiting + body size | | `server/test/health.test.mjs` | Create — TDD tests for enhanced health/ready/live/metrics/CORS | | `server/test/server.test.mjs` | Modify — fix `deepStrictEqual(body, { ok: true })` → `strictEqual(body.ok, true)` | | `client/vite.config.js` | Modify — add `build.rollupOptions.output.manualChunks` | | `client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.jsx` | Create — class-based error boundary with retry | | `client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.test.jsx` | Create — TDD tests for error boundary | | `client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.jsx` | Create — reusable loading spinner | | `client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.css` | Create — spinner animation styles | | `client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.test.jsx` | Create — TDD tests for spinner | | `client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx` | Modify — replace loading div with `` | | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx` | Modify — lazy-load BlockEditor with `React.lazy` + `` | | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx` | Modify — wrap all renders in ``; add lazy mock test | | `client/src/App.jsx` | Modify — wrap `` in `` | | `client/src/App.test.jsx` | Modify — add ErrorBoundary integration tests |
